beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, check weather and transit alerts
beats.reasoning: Nia wakes at her usual time. Low energy from yesterday (45/100) means she moves slowly through morning routine. The transit strike is a significant disruption—she'll need to drive or rideshare to work, so she checks alerts and plans accordingly.

beats.activity: Drive to First Hill branch, arrive early for quiet prep time
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library First Hill Branch (First Hill)
beats.reasoning: Transit strike forces a car commute instead of her usual bus. She leaves early to avoid surge-priced rideshare and prefers the solitude of driving. 50-minute buffer accounts for possible traffic and her habit of arriving before opening.

beats.activity: Work shift: reference desk, teen book club planning, cataloging
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library First Hill Branch (First Hill)
beats.reasoning: Morning and early afternoon at the library. Mild weather and low energy suggest a steady, focused shift rather than rushed. She's comfortable in the library's cool, quiet environment.
Short walking commute to her go-to lunch spot. She's established this routine and the mild weather makes a short walk pleasant.

beats.activity: Lunch: iced lavender latte, light meal, people-watch
beats.reasoning: Nia repeats yesterday's lunch spot—she found it restorative. An hour gives her time to decompress, watch the lunch crowd, and recharge for the afternoon shift. Low energy means she values this quiet time.

beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library First Hill Branch (First Hill)
beats.reasoning: Short walk back. Mild weather and partly cloudy skies make this pleasant.

beats.activity: Afternoon work shift: reference desk, teen book club prep, acquisitions
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library First Hill Branch (First Hill)
beats.reasoning: Afternoon shift until just after transit strike ends at 4pm. She stays until 5:30pm to wrap up work and avoid the post-strike commute chaos.

beats.activity: Drive home from First Hill, listening to podcast or music
beats.reasoning: Evening drive home. Transit is back online by now, but she prefers the solo drive after a full day. 50-minute buffer for post-strike traffic settling.

beats.activity: Dinner: simple pasta, hydrate, settle into evening
beats.reasoning: Low energy and mild weather suggest a low-key dinner at home. She repeats the pattern from yesterday—simple, restorative food. No energy for going out.

beats.activity: Leisure: Stardew Valley, sort through film photos from recent hike, relax
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down. Low energy from yesterday carries into today. Nia gravitates toward her comfort activities: cozy gaming and reviewing her photography. Partly cloudy weather means no outdoor evening plans appeal.
8-hour sleep. A full, steady day without major disruptions leaves Nia satisfied but tired. She'll wake refreshed, hopefully with more energy for Friday.

diary:
The transit strike caught me off guard this morning—I'd checked the alerts but somehow didn't fully register it until I was already awake. Grateful I could drive in early to the First Hill branch; the quiet prep time before the chaos felt like a gift. The reference desk was steady, nothing overwhelming. I spent some time sketching out ideas for the teen book club—we're thinking graphic novels this month, which always gets them excited.

Lunch at Voila was perfect timing. The lavender latte hit different today, and I just sat watching the usual First Hill crowd drift by. Back at the desk, acquisitions kept me busy enough to zone out a bit. The drive home felt longer than usual—not traffic, just my own headspace, I think. By evening I was deep in Stardew Valley, sorting through those photos from the Rattlesnake Lake hike last weekend. The light was golden that day. Everything feels a little slower tonight, which is exactly what I needed.
thought_process: The transit strike barely disrupted my day since I drive, but it did make me hyperaware of privilege—I saw the worry on some coworkers' faces about getting home. I chose to drive in early partly out of habit, partly because I knew the branch would be understaffed if people couldn't get in. The teen book club planning energized me; it's where I feel most purposeful at work. Lunch was deliberately solo time to decompress before the afternoon push. By evening, I was emotionally spent in that good way—the podcast during my drive home helped me transition, and switching to Stardew Valley was the right call. I needed to create something small and controllable after a day of managing other people's information needs.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
